steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to explain whom the award is named for. Only one note answers this directly: 'It is named after groundbreaking British mathematician Alan Turing.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In 1994, Raj Reddy won the A.M. Turing Award for pioneering the development of large-scale artificial intelligence systems.: This tells us who won the award and why — not whom it is named for. ✗
  • Choice B — The A.M. Turing Award is given for "major contributions of lasting importance to computing.": This describes the award's criteria, not its namesake. ✗
  • Choice C — The A.M. Turing Award is named for groundbreaking British mathematician Alan Turing.: This directly states whom the award is named for, matching the student's goal. ✓
  • Choice D — It was in 1994 that Raj Reddy won the A.M. Turing Award.: This emphasizes when Raj Reddy won, not whom the award is named for. ✗
C is correct.
